patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,7 @@
 # RGE Handoff Document
 
+> **Snapshot**: 2026-06-02 (MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A2 — Edit menu landed; only View/Play menus remain deferred): #295 (`0bc6a0c`, MENUREGISTRY-EDITMENU-A2) added an **Edit** menu (Undo / Redo) to the host menu bar, driven by the same W08 `MenuRegistry` path as File — `EguiHost::new` declares `editor.main_menu.edit`, registers Undo / Redo as `MenuEntry`s, resolves once (one registry, both points), and the editor-shell drain routes `Command::Undo` / `Command::Redo` to `EditorShell::undo_command` / `redo_command`, behavior-identical to the existing `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y` keystrokes. This supersedes the A1 snapshot below, which listed the Edit menu as deferred. STILL DEFERRED (narrowed further): the **View** menu (needs a NEW camera-reset action first, e.g. via `isometric_camera_for_bounds`) and the **Play** menu (needs only menu/UI wiring, NOT a new action — `Command::PlayStart` / `PlayStop` / `PlayPause` / `PlayStep` already exist and the PIE path is already runtime-wired through `EditorShell::handle_button`, Phase 5.3 CLOSED, so the Play menu is pure FIFO→`handle_button` wiring and is the intended next feature beat); registry/accelerator-driven command execution (clicks still use the FIFO), accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population, plugin entries, dynamic predicates, and per-frame re-resolve. The A1 snapshot below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.
+
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-02 (MENUREGISTRY-FILEBAR-A1-DOC-RECONCILE — A1 landed, File menu entries are now `MenuRegistry::resolve`-produced): #291 (`79fa41b`) changed `editor-egui-host` so `EguiHost::new` builds the File menu through the W08 `MenuRegistry`: declare `editor.main_menu.file`, register Open / Save / Save As New Project as `MenuEntry`s, resolve with an empty `PredicateContext`, and cache the resolved `(label, Command)` pairs. The rendered File menu remains behavior-identical: same labels, same order, same host->shell FIFO, same editor-shell routing from #288. This supersedes the #287/#288 snapshot below that described the File bar as a direct host menu over a hardcoded item list with `MenuRegistry::resolve` deferred. STILL DEFERRED (narrowed): registry/accelerator-driven command execution (File clicks still use the FIFO), Edit/View/Play menus, accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population, plugin entries, dynamic predicates, and per-frame re-resolve. Older dated 2026-06-01 / 2026-05-28 audit lines that say no functional `MenuRegistry::resolve` are historical and are not rewritten in place.
 
 > **AI Handoff Protocol**: AI-to-AI dispatch packets (Planner / Executor / Reviewer with human arbitration) follow the append-only governance protocol in [`ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md`](ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md). Templates: [`ai_handoffs/templates/`](ai_handoffs/templates/). The protocol is now also runtime-automated: `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` orchestrates one bounded dispatch (Codex plans / Claude executes / Codex controls, with Claude's `GATE_VERDICT` plan-gate marker and `EXEC_STATUS` / `EXEC_PACKET` execute markers driving control flow), and `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` is the GitHub issue queue runner — it pulls the oldest open `ai-dispatch`-labelled issue, runs it through the loop on a per-issue `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-<n>` branch, writes a timestamped `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` audit file, and auto-publishes only when the loop exits 0 and Codex control says `pass`; failed or blocked runs remain local and are labelled failed. The recurring `/loop` queue cadence is semi-attended and bound to the local authenticated CLI session, not a headless cloud daemon — see [`AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`](AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md). The packets themselves remain a documentation convention, not a replacement for ADRs / `Status.md` / `HANDOFF.md` / `change.md`. Precedent files at the repo root (`OPENAItoCLAUDE_*.md`, `CLAUDEtoOPENAI_*.md`, `CLAUDE_SUB_EPSILON_REVIEW.md`) remain valid historical records and are not migrated.

@@ -1650,3 +1650,5 @@ ew / partial / is_full_revolution / existing geometry tests. **Critical design c
 2026-05-23 15:20:00 - REPO TRANSFER (operational, not a dispatch). Repository moved from `CADRust/RGE` to `RustCADs/RGE` to escape the exhausted Actions minutes on the CADRust org (3,000 min/month allotment hit, ~87% consumed by sibling `CADRust/RCAD` repo per metered-usage view). GitHub auto-redirects the old `CADRust/RGE` URL for ~1 year; local `origin` remote updated from `https://github.com/CADRust/RGE.git` to `https://github.com/RustCADs/RGE.git` via `git remote set-url` + `git fetch origin` + `git remote prune origin` (17 stale `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-*` remote-tracking refs pruned). **All carried over via transfer semantics**: ~130 issues (including the 100 `ai-auto`-labelled issues that ISSUE-128's cap-stop-state audit reached), ~30 PRs, all labels (`ai-auto` / `ai-dispatch-done` / `ai-dispatch-failed` / `ai-dispatch-retry` / `ai-dispatch`), releases, branches, tags, and all 6 GitHub Actions workflow definitions (Format check, Architecture lints, Workspace tests, Script bench compile, Supply chain (cargo-deny), CodeQL). **Visibility changed**: `CADRust/RGE` was flipped `private → public` mid-transfer because RustCADs free-tier policy required a public source for the incoming transfer; reverting to private at the new home is now an operator decision (not done in this commit). **CI verified executing** on RustCADs runners: the fmt rerun `#26332202630` on commit `2ce4404` completed `success` (was `completed/failure` on CADRust); CodeQL auto-triggered as `#26336370076` and progressed real steps with `runner_name: "GitHub Actions 1000000000"` populated and `steps_count: 10/11` recorded. This is the inverse of the ISSUE-100 (task #8) CI audit's documented "empty runner_name, 0 steps, ~2-second failure" pattern — that pattern is fully resolved on RustCADs because the org has its own Actions-minutes allotment uncoupled from `CADRust/RCAD`'s monthly burn. Reruns of `#26332202631` (Architecture lints), `#26332202627` (Workspace tests), `#26332202626` (Script bench compile), and `#26332202614` (Supply chain) all triggered cleanly within seconds — 3 of 4 had runners attached within 15 s, the 4th queued normally. **Freeze posture unchanged**: scheduler `RGE-AiDispatch` remains `Disabled`, `ai-auto` cap remains hard-frozen at 100 in both `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1` `[ValidateRange(1, 100)]`, no task #23 seeded, no autonomous work since #22 / ISSUE-128. **No script edits required**: dispatch automation scripts auto-detect repo slug via the `gh` CLI, so no `Invoke-AiDispatch*.ps1` / `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1` / `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` / workflow-file edits were needed; the only operational change is the local `origin` URL plus this `change.md` entry. **Historical immutability preserved**: prior dated `change.md` entries — including the 2026-05-08 "first public commit pushed to CADRust/RGE on GitHub" record at line 672 — and all `ai_handoffs/` packets (TASK / EXEC / etc.) remain unedited as immutable historical records; they were accurate at the time and the GitHub redirect keeps their `CADRust/RGE` URLs resolvable. **Hard non-goals observed**: NO source / test / fixture / Cargo / lint / ADR / workflow / script / schema edits; NO dispatch packet rewrites; NO `ai-auto` cap change; NO scheduler re-enable; NO retroactive replacement of `CADRust/RGE` references in `ai_handoffs/` / `ai_dispatch_logs/` / earlier `change.md` entries. **Verification (docs-only gates)**: `git diff --name-only` lists exactly `change.md`; `git status --short --untracked-files=all` shows no untracked files; local `git fetch origin` resolves against `https://github.com/RustCADs/RGE.git` and `origin/main` HEAD matches `2ce4404`. Files modified (1 doc total): `change.md` (this entry appended; no rewrite of any prior entry, no edit to any other doc). **Three required references present in this entry**: GitHub commit `2ce4404`, GitHub Actions run `#26332202630` (Format check rerun on RustCADs/RGE), and `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-100_EXEC_2026-05-23_00-37-25+0300.md` (the original CI failure-boundary audit that diagnosed the empty-runner pattern this transfer resolves).
 
 2026-06-02 (MENUREGISTRY-FILEBAR-A1-DOC-RECONCILE — planning-substrate hygiene after A1, docs-only). Forward-only reconciliation that #291 (`79fa41b`, MENUREGISTRY-FILEBAR-A1) landed the previously deferred File-menu item production through `MenuRegistry::resolve`. The File bar remains behavior-identical: `editor-egui-host` declares `editor.main_menu.file`, registers Open / Save / Save As New Project as `MenuEntry`s, resolves against empty `PredicateContext`, caches the resolved `(label, Command)` pairs in `EguiHost::new`, and still pushes the same `Command` values through the existing host->shell FIFO that #288 routes in editor-shell. This entry plus top snapshots in `Status.md` / `HANDOFF.md` and a new `plans/BASELINE.md` 2026-06-02 subsection supersede the now-stale #287/#288 "direct host menu bar over a hardcoded item list / MenuRegistry::resolve deferred" framing. **Still deferred (narrowed):** registry/accelerator-driven command execution (File clicks still use FIFO), Edit/View/Play menus, accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population, plugin entries, dynamic predicates, per-frame re-resolve, plus non-menu audit gaps (drag-drop, `io-image`, World-only Command-Bus `Action` context). **History preserved:** older 2026-06-01 / 2026-05-28 audit lines that say no functional `MenuRegistry::resolve` remain as dated records and are superseded forward, not rewritten. **Scope:** docs-only; edits confined to `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`; no Rust/test/Cargo/automation changes. Verification: `git diff --check` clean; grep confirms no living current snapshot still says the File bar is hardcoded while `MenuRegistry::resolve` is deferred.
+
+2026-06-02 (MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A2 — planning-substrate hygiene after A2 + menu rustdoc + lint-doc counts, docs-only). Forward-only reconciliation after #295 (`0bc6a0c`, MENUREGISTRY-EDITMENU-A2) added the **Edit** menu (Undo / Redo) via `MenuRegistry`. The host builds both menus from one registry (`build_main_menu_entries` declares `editor.main_menu.file` + `editor.main_menu.edit`, resolves once, caches both lists; `render` paints a second `menu_button("Edit")`); `editor-shell` `drain_and_route_menu_commands` routes `Command::Undo` / `Command::Redo` to `EditorShell::undo_command` / `redo_command`, behavior-identical to `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y`. This entry + new top snapshots in `Status.md` / `HANDOFF.md` + a new `plans/BASELINE.md` A2 subsection supersede the now-stale "Edit menu deferred" framing from the #292 / A1 reconcile (which predated A2). **Also folded in (post-2026-06-02 deep-audit doc hygiene):** fixed stale menu rustdoc in `crates/editor-egui-host/src/{lib.rs,handoff.rs}` (the "Dispatch A only enqueues — nothing drains yet" claim was false since #288 added the drain; the "the File menu bar" framing widened to "File + Edit"); and corrected VERIFIED-stale counts in `docs/§18/ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md` — forbidden-dep **6 → 7 rules** (Rule 7 `editor-shell` loader-free landed #280), exemptions **60 → 28** total (all failure-class rollout-debt; **28 remain** of the original 81; no graph-foundation or reserved entries — verified with anchored `grep -c '^\[\[exemption\]\]'`, since the unanchored form over-counts by matching the file's schema comment). **Still deferred (narrowed):** the View menu (needs a NEW camera-reset action first) and the Play menu (needs only menu/UI wiring — Play is already runtime-wired via `EditorShell::handle_button`, with `Command::PlayStart` / `PlayStop` / `PlayPause` / `PlayStep` variants present, so it is pure FIFO→`handle_button` wiring with no new action, the intended next feature beat), accelerator-table execution/display/conflict, plugin entries, dynamic predicates, per-frame re-resolve. **History preserved:** the A1 (#292) reconcile entry above + all older dated entries are byte-identical (forward-only). **Scope:** docs + rustdoc/comments only — edits confined to `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`, `crates/editor-egui-host/src/{lib.rs,handoff.rs}`, `docs/§18/ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md`; no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo / routing change. Provenance: the 2026-06-02 post-menu-arc read-only 5-axis deep audit (health strong; authority intact; no kernel-cavity pressure). Gate: VERIFY OK 7/7 + 10/10 arch lints.

@@ -73,11 +73,11 @@ const MENU_COMMAND_QUEUE_CAP: usize = 64;

     queue: Mutex<VecDeque<Command>>,

@@ -2,10 +2,10 @@
 
 | Companion to | PLAN.md §1.3 (Rule 3 — line cap, no utils) + §1.6.4 (one-import-path-per-format) + §1.8 (forbidden-dep DAG) + §1.13 (failure-class taxonomy) + §1.14 (graph-foundation substrate) + §1.15 (editor-state coordination-not-authority) + §6.16 (Command Bus); IMPLEMENTATION.md Phase 0.2 (architecture-lints DONE) |
 |---|---|
-| Status | Active v1; the 9 enforcement lints all PASS (per Status.md 2026-05-09 architecture lint matrix) + the supplementary `snapshot-participate` (warning-level, never fails CI); 60 entries in `exemptions.toml` (1 graph-foundation false-positive + 58 failure-class rollout-debt + 1 reserved); 107 tests across `tools/architecture-lints/{src,tests}/` (38 inline + 69 fixture-based integration); CI-wired via `.github/workflows/architecture.yml`; the workspace's only architectural-correctness gate today |
+| Status | Active v1; the 9 enforcement lints all PASS (per Status.md 2026-05-09 architecture lint matrix) + the supplementary `snapshot-participate` (warning-level, never fails CI); 28 entries in `exemptions.toml` (all failure-class rollout-debt; no graph-foundation or reserved entries); 121 tests across `tools/architecture-lints/{src,tests}/` (43 inline + 78 fixture-based integration); CI-wired via `.github/workflows/architecture.yml`; the workspace's only architectural-correctness gate today |
 | Audience | Subsystem authors landing first real implementation (must not break a lint); reviewers verifying which rule a violation maps to; orchestrator authors adding a new lint; rollout-debt tracker authors clearing exemptions |
 | Sibling doc | `GRAPH_FOUNDATION.md` — substrate behind the graph-foundation lint Check 2; `RECOVERY_MODEL.md` — the failure-class lint's enforcement target; `EXECUTION_DOMAINS.md` — kernel-isolation rule's domain context; `EDITOR_STATE_MODEL.md` — editor-state-ownership rule's owner |
-| Reference impls | `tools/architecture-lints/src/main.rs` (108L; CLI dispatch) · `tools/architecture-lints/src/common.rs` (250L; `Violation` / `LintReport` / `Exemptions` / cargo-metadata helpers) · 10 lint implementation modules (9 enforcement + the supplementary `snapshot-participate`) · 10 fixture-based integration test files at `tools/architecture-lints/tests/` · `tools/architecture-lints/exemptions.toml` (60 entries) · `.github/workflows/architecture.yml` (CI invocation) |
+| Reference impls | `tools/architecture-lints/src/main.rs` (121L; CLI dispatch) · `tools/architecture-lints/src/common.rs` (250L; `Violation` / `LintReport` / `Exemptions` / cargo-metadata helpers) · 10 lint implementation modules (9 enforcement + the supplementary `snapshot-participate`) · 10 fixture-based integration test files at `tools/architecture-lints/tests/` · `tools/architecture-lints/exemptions.toml` (28 entries — all failure-class rollout-debt; no graph-foundation or reserved) · `.github/workflows/architecture.yml` (CI invocation) |
 
 > Convention defined by `PLUGIN_HOST_PATTERNS.md` §header. This doc is the meta-reference for the architecture-enforcement suite — 9 enforcement lints plus the supplementary warning-level `snapshot-participate`. Each lint's specific rationale is in its module-doc; this doc covers the suite-level shape, the exemptions-toml registry, the CI integration, and lint-author guidance for adding a new lint.
 
@@ -17,15 +17,15 @@ PLAN's architectural rules are spread across §1.3, §1.6.4, §1.8, §1.13, §1.
 
 - **One CLI binary, 9 enforcement lints + 1 supplementary, single PASS/FAIL gate.** `cargo run -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` runs every lint and exits non-zero if any *enforcement* lint failed (the supplementary `snapshot-participate` always exits 0). CI's `.github/workflows/architecture.yml` wires this exactly.
 - **One exemptions registry, code-review-trailed.** `tools/architecture-lints/exemptions.toml` is the only place to suppress a lint against a specific file path; adding an entry requires explicit reason field + follow-up plan. No inline `#[allow(...)]` annotations scattered across the workspace.
-- **Per-lint fixture-based integration tests.** Every lint has its own `tests/<lint>_test.rs` exercising synthetic fixtures against the lint's algorithm; `tests/fixtures/forbidden_dep/` holds workspace-shaped fixture trees. Test count today: 107 across all lints.
+- **Per-lint fixture-based integration tests.** Every lint has its own `tests/<lint>_test.rs` exercising synthetic fixtures against the lint's algorithm; `tests/fixtures/forbidden_dep/` holds workspace-shaped fixture trees. Integration-test count today: 78 across all lints.
 
 ## 2. The 10 lints — what each enforces
 
 The canonical mapping (per `main.rs` lines 38-67 and Status.md "Architecture lint matrix"):
 
 | Lint | PLAN.md rule | What it detects |
 |---|---|---|
-| `forbidden-dep` | §1.8 | The 6-rule dependency DAG (Tier-1↛Tier-2; Tier-2↛Tier-3; cad-core stands alone; editor-ui↛physics/audio/input; physics↛script-host; renderers↛game-domain) |
+| `forbidden-dep` | §1.8 | The 7-rule dependency DAG (Tier-1↛Tier-2; Tier-2↛Tier-3; cad-core stands alone; editor-ui↛physics/audio/input; physics↛script-host; renderers↛game-domain; editor-shell stays loader-free) |
 | `split-exemption` | §1.3 Rule 3 | Any `.rs` >1000 lines requires a `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION: <reason>` annotation |
 | `no-utils` | §1.3 Rule 3 | No `utils.rs` / `util.rs` / `helpers.rs` / `helper.rs` files anywhere in the workspace |
 | `graph-foundation` | §1.14 | Check 1: no `NodeId` / `EdgeId` / `StableHash` redefinitions outside `kernel/graph-foundation`. Check 2: no `BTreeMap<K, BTreeSet<K>>` / `HashMap<K, HashSet<K>>` adjacency reinvention |
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@ The canonical mapping (per `main.rs` lines 38-67 and Status.md "Architecture lin
 
 For each lint, the algorithm sketch + what counts as a violation. Full module-docs in `tools/architecture-lints/src/<lint>.rs`.
 
-### `forbidden-dep` (`forbidden_dep.rs`, 699L, 22 inline tests)
+### `forbidden-dep` (`forbidden_dep.rs`, 817L, 27 inline tests)
 
 Walks `cargo metadata`'s direct workspace-internal dep graph (external registry deps ignored). For each rule, classifies each (consumer, dep) pair via `Tier::One` / `Tier::Two` / `Tier::Three` and checks the prohibition. Workspace package names carry the `rge-` prefix (`rge-cad-core`, `rge-physics`, etc.) — audit-6 (2026-05-09) caught that rules 3-6 were dead code because they compared against bare names; the fix was prefix-correct comparisons (per `forbidden_dep.rs` lines 14-22). The forbidden-dep `rge-` prefix discipline is now active per audit-5 closure.
 
@@ -61,9 +61,9 @@ Two checks per `graph_foundation.rs` lines 1-24:
 - **Check 1 (forbidden-name redefinition).** No crate outside `kernel/graph-foundation/` may define its own `NodeId` / `EdgeId` / `StableHash` (struct, enum, type alias, or trait). Uses `syn::visit` to inspect AST definitions.
 - **Check 2 (adjacency-map reinvention).** Added 2026-05-09 per audit-5 deep-audit followup. No crate outside `kernel/graph-foundation/` may define a struct field of shape `BTreeMap<K, BTreeSet<K>>` or `HashMap<K, HashSet<K>>` where the outer key type equals the inner set's element type. That shape is the canonical "I'm reinventing graph storage" pattern. Without Check 2, audit-1 would have missed `kernel/asset::DependencyGraph` silently rolling its own graph.
 
-The single substantive `graph-foundation` exemption (per `exemptions.toml` lines 17-31) is `crates/editor-ui/src/layout/node.rs` — false-positive: editor-ui's `LayoutNodeId` is its own UI-tree-node identifier, semantically unrelated to graph-foundation's `NodeId`.
+`exemptions.toml` currently carries **no** `graph-foundation` exemption (all 28 entries are `failure-class` rollout-debt). A prior false-positive exemption for `crates/editor-ui/src/layout/node.rs` — editor-ui's `LayoutNodeId` is its own UI-tree-node identifier, semantically unrelated to graph-foundation's `NodeId` — is no longer present in the registry.
 
-### `editor-state-ownership` (`editor_state_ownership.rs`, 353L, 0 inline tests; 7 integration tests)
+### `editor-state-ownership` (`editor_state_ownership.rs`, 500L, 0 inline tests; 16 integration tests)
 
 Two-part per the module-doc lines 1-22:
 
@@ -93,19 +93,18 @@ The lint that closes the audit-1 audit-debt registry per `RECOVERY_MODEL.md` §5
 
 Closed set: `recoverable` / `snapshot-recoverable` / `plugin-fatal` / `session-fatal` / `kernel-fatal` (case-sensitive). Multi-value `//! Failure class: recoverable, snapshot-recoverable` is supported.
 
-### `snapshot-participate` (`snapshot_participate.rs`, 378L, 5 inline tests; 5 integration tests) — supplementary / warning-level
+### `snapshot-participate` (`snapshot_participate.rs`, 377L, 5 inline tests; 5 integration tests) — supplementary / warning-level
 
 NOT an enforcement lint. For every Tier-2 crate in the closed `STATEFUL_TIER2_CRATES` list (`cad-core`, `cad-projection`, `physics` + the forward-compat `particles` / `sculpt`), checks whether its `src/` tree contains an `impl SnapshotParticipate` (string match — the trait name is unique to this codebase, so no `syn` walk is needed). Emits an `info:` line per crate (impl present → stdout; missing → stderr) plus a one-line coverage summary, then reports PASS **regardless** — it NEVER pushes a `Violation`, so its exit code is always 0 and it cannot fail the `all` aggregate. Scaffolds the PLAN §13.2 v1.0 gate ("all stateful Tier-2 has `SnapshotParticipate`") as coverage tracking without blocking inter-Phase landings; a future dispatch flips it to error-level by pushing a `Violation` for the missing-impl case (per the `snapshot_participate.rs` module-doc "Why warning-level only").

 The fixture pattern: write a synthetic `.rs` file containing the rule-violating shape, parse it into the lint's algorithm, assert the algorithm reports the expected `Violation` (file path, line, message). Each lint test exercises the positive-case (rule violated → violation reported) AND the negative-case (rule satisfied → no violation reported); the `forbidden_dep` lint additionally exercises `tests/fixtures/forbidden_dep/` synthetic workspace trees against `cargo_metadata::MetadataCommand`.

+### 2026-06-02 — Edit menu now registry-produced (#295 A2)
+
+**Forward-only follow-up (MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A2).** Supersedes the A1 subsection below, which listed the Edit menu among the deferred items. A2 landed on main at `0bc6a0c` via PR #295.
+
+**Now CLOSED — the Edit menu (Undo / Redo).** `crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` builds BOTH menus from a single `MenuRegistry`: `build_main_menu_entries()` (renamed from `build_file_menu_entries`) declares `editor.main_menu.file` + `editor.main_menu.edit`, registers Open/Save/Save-As + Undo/Redo, resolves once against an empty `PredicateContext`, and caches both resolved `(label, Command)` lists on `EguiHost`; `render()` paints a second `menu_button("Edit")`. `editor-shell` `drain_and_route_menu_commands` routes `Command::Undo` / `Command::Redo` to `EditorShell::undo_command` / `redo_command`, mirroring the `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y` keystroke path exactly (swallowing `NothingToUndo` / `NothingToRedo`) — behavior-identical to the keyboard route.
+
+**Still open — explicitly NOT closed here (narrowed from the A1 list):**
+- **View / Play menu breadth** remains unbuilt, for DIFFERENT reasons. **View → Reset Camera** needs a NEW action first (e.g. via `isometric_camera_for_bounds`). **Play does NOT need a new action** — `Command::PlayStart` / `PlayStop` / `PlayPause` / `PlayStep` already exist (`crates/editor-ui/src/menus/command.rs`) and the PIE path is already runtime-wired through `EditorShell::handle_button` (Phase 5.3 CLOSED — see the PIE round-trip baseline below); the Play menu is therefore pure FIFO→`handle_button` menu/UI wiring, the intended next feature beat. The A2 scoping criterion was: wire only items with BOTH a `Command` variant AND a reachable shell action — Undo/Redo were the only two scoped into A2 (Play also satisfies that criterion via `handle_button` and is next).
+- accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population, plugin menu entries, dynamic predicates, and per-frame re-resolve remain unbuilt.
+- Generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution remains deferred (menu clicks still flow through the host→shell FIFO + `editor-shell` routing, not a registry/accelerator execution path).
+
+**Historical preservation.** The A1 subsection below and all earlier dated entries are preserved byte-identical as dated history; their "Edit … deferred/unbuilt" lines are superseded forward by this subsection, not rewritten in place (the A1 still-open bullet remains exactly as it was at A1).
+
+**Scope:** docs + menu rustdoc/comments + `docs/§18/ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md` count fixes; no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo / routing change.
+
